acceptedAnswer.text: Not mandatory by law, but increasingly required by: government tenders (ISO 9001), IT clients (ISO 27001), food industry (ISO 22000), and export buyers.

mainEntity.name: How long does ISO certification take?

acceptedAnswer.text: 2-4 months depending on company size, existing processes, and certification scope.

mainEntity.name: Which ISO certification does my business need?

acceptedAnswer.text: ISO 9001: Quality Management (most common). ISO 14001: Environmental Management. ISO 27001: Information Security. ISO 22000: Food Safety. ISO 45001: Occupational Health & Safety. Selection depends on industry and client requirements.

mainEntity.name: How much does ISO certification cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Consultancy: ₹25,000-₹75,000. Certification body audit: ₹30,000-₹1,00,000. Total: ₹55,000-₹1,75,000 depending on standard, scope, and organisation size. Annual surveillance audit: ₹20,000-₹50,000.

mainEntity.name: How long is ISO certificate valid?

acceptedAnswer.text: 3 years. Annual surveillance audits required (Year 1 and Year 2). Recertification audit in Year 3 for renewal. Non-compliance during surveillance: certificate can be suspended or withdrawn.
